name: Reclick
description:
Records your practice-mode clicks into .gdph files and shows them as guide bars on the level. Bars auto-hide when you quit or switch to a different level; you can also Unload them manually from the pause menu. Never presses anything for you.

<cr>Note from the author:</c> using Reclick to learn a level by following bars from your own past attempts is, in my opinion, NOT a legitimate clear. If you publish a record of a level using a Reclick recording you made yourself, you are misrepresenting your skill.

The intended workflow is: record a macro in xdBot, replay it in xdBot to generate the .gdph, then use the .gdph as a visual study aid only. The mod itself never presses any buttons; what you do with the bars is up to you and your community's standards.

auto-save.name: Auto-save on level end / exit
auto-save.description: Automatically save a .gdph file in <GD>/gdph-recordings/ when a level is completed or you quit with clicks recorded.

display-mode.name: Display mode
display-mode.description:
How click events are drawn over the level.

<cy>Bars</c> — wide rectangles for holds, thin lines for taps. Best for ship/wave/UFO sections.

<cy>Marks only</c> — only a thin line at the moment each click STARTS. Best for cube sections where holding doesn't matter.

<cy>Start+End marks</c> — thin lines at both the start AND end of every click, no rectangles between.

tap-color.name: Tap / start mark color
tap-color.description: Color for short taps and for start marks in 'Marks only' / 'Start+End' modes.

tap-alpha.name: Tap / start mark opacity
tap-alpha.description: How visible tap and start-mark bars are. 0 = invisible, 255 = solid.

hold-color.name: Hold / end mark color
hold-color.description: Color for hold rectangles (in Bars mode) or end marks (in Start+End mode).

hold-alpha.name: Hold / end mark opacity
hold-alpha.description: How visible hold rectangles and end marks are. 0 = invisible, 255 = solid.

hold-threshold.name: Hold threshold (px)
hold-threshold.description: Width in world pixels above which a click is considered a hold rather than a tap. Only used in 'Bars' mode.
